description: BuildPact — Universal Spec-Driven Development Framework. Full pipeline from Foundation through AutoResearch.

userStories.title: Epic 1.1: Project Initialization via CLI
userStories.description: As a framework user, I want to run `npx buildpact init <project-name>` and complete a guided TUI setup so that I have a complete IDE-specific project structure ready in under 2 minutes.
acceptanceCriteria: npx buildpact init <name> launches bilingual TUI with language, domain, IDE, experience, and Squad selection
All selected IDE config files generated (.claude/commands/, .cursor/rules/, .gemini/, .codex/)
.buildpact/ directory created with constitution.md scaffold, config.yaml, project-context.md, audit/
Installation completes within 60 seconds
Offline fallback uses bundled Software Squad when remote fetch fails
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed in Epic 1. All 6 stories done.

userStories.title: Epic 1.2: Diagnostic Health Check
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run `buildpact doctor` to check Node.js, Git, IDE configs, Squad integrity, and .buildpact/ consistency with bilingual output.
acceptanceCriteria: buildpact doctor checks Node.js ≥20.x, Git availability, IDE config validity, Squad integrity, .buildpact/ consistency
Output in configured language (PT-BR or EN) with PASS/WARN/FAIL per check
Actionable remediation steps for each failure
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed in Epic 1.

userStories.title: Epic 1.3: Subagent Isolation Architecture
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all heavy computation delegated to isolated subagents via Task() dispatch with clean context windows and ≤20KB payloads.
acceptanceCriteria: buildTaskPayload() assembles minimal TaskDispatchPayload with unique taskId
Subagents receive only task-specific payload, not accumulated orchestrator context
validatePayloadSize() enforces ≤20KB payload limit (NFR-02)
All orchestrator commands ≤300 lines and ≤15% of model context window
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed in Epic 1.

userStories.title: Epic 1.4: Real-Time Context & Cost Monitoring
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a real-time status bar showing context usage with WARNING (>50%) and CRITICAL (>75%) alerts plus estimated cost per phase.
acceptanceCriteria: getContextUsage() returns current context percentage
checkContextAlert() returns WARNING at >50%, CRITICAL at >75%
getCostState() returns estimated cost for current phase and accumulated milestone cost
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed in Epic 1.

userStories.title: Epic 1.5: Automatic Document Sharding
userStories.description: As a developer, I want documents exceeding 500 lines auto-split into atomic files with an index.md, achieving ≥70% token savings.
acceptanceCriteria: shouldShard() triggers at 500-line threshold
splitIntoSections() splits by level-2 headings into atomic shards
buildShardManifest() generates index.md with navigation links
Token savings ≥70% when loading individual shards vs monolithic file
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed in Epic 1.

userStories.title: Epic 1.6: Project Decision Log
userStories.description: As a developer, I want DECISIONS.md (append-only) and STATUS.md (living state) maintained in project root for AI session continuity.
acceptanceCriteria: appendDecision() appends entries with date, decision, rationale, affected artifacts
DECISIONS.md is append-only — never overwrites previous entries
STATUS.md template created during init
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed in Epic 1.

userStories.title: Epic 2.1: Create and Edit Project Constitution
userStories.description: As a tech lead, I want to run /bp:constitution to create or update immutable project rules (coding standards, compliance, architecture, quality gates, domain rules) saved to .buildpact/constitution.md.
acceptanceCriteria: Guided TUI flow collects 5 sections: coding standards, compliance, architecture, quality gates, domain rules
Result saved to .buildpact/constitution.md
Edit mode loads existing constitution and allows targeted section edits
Constitution path injected into TaskDispatchPayload for subagent context
Bilingual support (EN + PT-BR) via i18n keys
Audit logging on create/update
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed and code-reviewed. 3 MEDIUM issues fixed.

userStories.title: Epic 2.2: Automatic Constitution Enforcement
userStories.description: As a developer, I want every specify/plan/execute action to automatically validate output against the Constitution, generating warnings for violations.
acceptanceCriteria: Constitution auto-injected into subagent context on specify, plan, execute commands
Output validated against Constitution rules before acceptance
Violations generate warnings referencing specific violated principle by name
Constitution modification always requires explicit user consent (NFR-25)
Beginner mode shows plain-language warnings with no jargon
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. constitution-enforcer.ts with extractPrinciples/validateOutput/formatViolationWarning/resolveConstitutionPath. Consent prompt added to handler.

userStories.title: Epic 2.3: Constitution Versioning & Change Tracking
userStories.description: As a tech lead, I want every Constitution change to generate an update checklist tracking what changed, why, and which downstream artifacts need review.
acceptanceCriteria: constitution_update_checklist.md generated on Constitution modification
Checklist tracks: what changed, stated reason, downstream artifacts needing review
Lists all existing specs and plans referencing changed principles with recommended review action
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. constitution-versioner.ts with diffConstitutionPrinciples/scanDownstreamArtifacts/buildChecklistContent/writeUpdateChecklist. Handler extended to ask reason and generate constitution_update_checklist.md on every edit.

userStories.title: Epic 3.1: Quick Command — Zero-Ceremony Execution
userStories.description: As a developer, I want /bp:quick <description> to go from natural language to atomic commit in under 5 minutes, skipping all planning ceremony.
acceptanceCriteria: /bp:quick generates minimal spec, executes change, produces one atomic Git commit
Flow completes in under 5 minutes
Constitution validated even in quick mode
Commit message format: type(quick): description
Exactly one commit in Git history per quick execution
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. handler.ts with inferCommitType/buildQuickSpec/runGit. Args or TUI prompt → spec saved to .buildpact/specs/<slug>/quick-spec.md → git add + git commit type(quick): description. Constitution path injected when available.

userStories.title: Epic 3.2: Quick Flow with Lightweight Context Gathering
userStories.description: As a developer, I want /bp:quick --discuss to ask 3-5 targeted questions before execution without entering the full planning pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: /bp:quick --discuss asks 3-5 focused clarifying questions
Each question presents numbered options with 'Other (free text)' as final choice
After answers, proceeds directly to execution (no full plan/specify pipeline)
Output reflects specific user answers, not generic assumptions
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. gatherDiscussContext() in handler.ts asks 4 questions (scope, risk, done, constraints) via clack.select with an 'other' free-text fallback. Answers embedded in spec under 'Context from Discussion'. 6 new unit tests. 232 total tests passing, zero typecheck errors.

userStories.title: Epic 3.3: Quick Flow with Plan Verification
userStories.description: As a developer, I want /bp:quick --full to add plan-checking and verification for higher-risk small changes.
acceptanceCriteria: /bp:quick --full generates plan, validates with 2-perspective check, executes, and verifies
If verification fails, fix plan auto-generated and offered
User notified of risks before execution with abort/continue option
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. buildFullPlan/validatePlanCompleteness/validatePlanFeasibility/verifyAgainstSpec/buildFixPlan in handler.ts. --full flag: generates plan.md, 2-perspective validation (completeness + feasibility), risk confirm before commit, verification → fix-plan.md on failure. 16 new tests (pure functions + handler). 248 total tests passing, zero typecheck errors.

userStories.title: Epic 4.1: Natural Language Specification Capture
userStories.description: As a developer, I want /bp:specify to accept plain natural language input and produce a structured spec.md with user stories and acceptance criteria.
acceptanceCriteria: /bp:specify accepts natural language input, rejects implementation details at this stage
Beginner mode uses guided sequential wizard questions
Generated spec.md contains: user stories, ACs in Given/When/Then, FRs, NFRs, assumptions, Constitution self-assessment
Spec saved to .buildpact/specs/
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. detectImplementationDetails warns on implementation keywords. Beginner wizard (5 questions: persona/goal/motivation/success/constraints) generates structured spec.md with User Story, ACs in Given/When/Then, FRs, NFRs, Assumptions, Constitution Self-Assessment. Expert mode accepts NL input from args or TUI. Spec saved to .buildpact/specs/<slug>/spec.md. 18 tests (6 detectImplementationDetails, 5 buildSpecContent, 4 expert handler, 3 beginner handler). 266 total tests passing, zero typecheck errors.

userStories.title: Epic 4.2: Ambiguity Detection and Clarification Flow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the specify phase to detect ambiguities and offer structured choices with ≥3 numbered options plus free text.
acceptanceCriteria: Ambiguity detection triggers clarification flow
Each ambiguity presents ≥3 numbered options with 'Other (free text)' as final choice
Selections incorporated into generated spec.md
Skips clarification if no ambiguities detected
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. detectAmbiguities() with 12 pattern rules (quickly/fast/real-time/easy/simple/secure/scalable/some/several/appropriate/modern/large). runClarificationFlow() presents ≥3 numbered options + 'Other (free text)' per ambiguity via clack.select/text. Answers embedded in spec.md under '## Clarifications' table. Flow skipped when no ambiguities detected. 283 tests passing, zero typecheck errors.

userStories.title: Epic 4.3: Domain-Aware Specification with Squad Integration
userStories.description: As a developer with an active Squad, I want /bp:specify to inject domain-specific question templates into the specify flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Active Squad injects domain-specific question templates into specify flow
Generated spec.md includes domain-specific constraints from Squad answers
Web Bundle mode uses Squad-defined conversational question flows instead of standard questions
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. readActiveSquad() reads active_squad from config.yaml + squad.yaml domain. getSquadQuestions() maps 4 domains (software/marketing/health/research) to 3 questions each with ≥3 options. runSquadFlow() uses clack.select (CLI) or clack.text (web bundle mode). readWebBundleMode() checks config mode: web-bundle. SpecInput.squadConstraints embedded in spec.md under ## Domain Constraints. 22 new tests. 305 total tests passing, zero typecheck errors.

userStories.title: Epic 4.4: Automation Maturity Assessment
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the specify phase to evaluate my task against a 5-stage automation maturity model and recommend the right level.
acceptanceCriteria: Advisor evaluates against 5 stages: Manual, Documented Skill, Alias, Heartbeat Check, Full Automation
Recommendation based on frequency, predictability, human decision requirements, cost-benefit
Recommendation included as section in spec.md with justification
User can override with override noted in spec
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. scoreMaturity() scores frequency+predictability+humanDecisions (0-9) → 5 stages. assessAutomationMaturity() uses 3 clack.select calls + override prompt. Recommendation and optional override written to spec.md under ## Automation Maturity Assessment. 14 new tests. 319 total tests passing, zero typecheck errors.

userStories.title: Epic 5.1: Automated Parallel Research Before Planning
userStories.description: As a developer, I want /bp:plan to spawn parallel research agents investigating domain, tech stack, and codebase before generating the plan.
acceptanceCriteria: Parallel research agents spawned for: tech stack, existing codebase, domain constraints from active Squad
Each research agent runs in isolated subagent context
Research results consolidated and used as plan generation input
Generated plan references specific findings from research
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. buildResearchPayload() builds isolated TaskDispatchPayload per topic. buildStubFindings() returns structured stub findings (Alpha). consolidateResearch() merges 3 results into ResearchSummary. buildPlanContent() generates plan.md with ## Research Findings, ## Key References from Research, ## Wave Plan. Handler writes to .buildpact/plans/<slug>/plan.md. 20 new tests. 339 total tests passing, zero typecheck errors.

userStories.title: Epic 5.2: Wave-Based Plan Generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want /bp:plan to analyze task dependencies and group them into sequential execution waves with parallel tasks within each wave.
acceptanceCriteria: Planner groups independent tasks into same wave, dependent tasks into subsequent waves
Vertical slices preferred over horizontal layers
Each plan file contains max 2-3 tasks; auto-splits if exceeded
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. extractTasksFromSpec() parses AC/FR bullets into PlanTask[]. inferDependencies() detects keyword-based deps. assignWaves() uses topological sort. groupIntoWaves() groups for parallel execution. splitWavesIfNeeded() auto-splits waves >2 tasks (plan-wave-1.md, plan-wave-1b.md, etc.). buildWaveFileContent() generates per-wave files. buildPlanContent() updated to show actual extracted waves. Handler writes multi-file output when needed. 25 new tests. 364 total tests passing, zero typecheck errors.

userStories.title: Epic 5.3: Model Profile Configuration
userStories.description: As a tech lead, I want to configure quality/balanced/budget model profiles with failover chains for cost optimization.
acceptanceCriteria: Model profiles configurable in config.yaml (quality/balanced/budget)
Pipeline commands use model specified for that phase in active profile
Automatic failover to next model in chain with retry delay and max wait
Operation-level routing within phases (e.g., research vs plan writing)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. parseModelProfiles() reads model_profile_* flat keys from config.yaml and merges with built-in defaults. MODEL_CATALOG maps 3 Anthropic models. readActiveProfileTier() reads active tier. resolveModelForOperation() routes ops to models. buildFailoverChain() builds primary+failover list. advanceFailover() advances with FAILOVER_EXHAUSTED error when exhausted. getCurrentModel() gets current. 39 new tests. 403 total tests passing, zero typecheck errors.

userStories.title: Epic 5.4: Nyquist Multi-Perspective Plan Validation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want plans validated by 3-4 independent analysis perspectives before execution begins.
acceptanceCriteria: 3-4 independent perspectives evaluate: completeness vs spec, internal consistency, dependency correctness, feasibility
Validation report lists issues per perspective
Critical issues block execution until resolved or overridden
User can request automatic plan revision for flagged issues
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. validateCompleteness (AC keyword coverage), validateConsistency (dup titles, short titles), validateDependencies (unknown/self deps), validateFeasibility (task count, vague language). validatePlan() aggregate. autoRevisePlan() heuristic fixer. formatValidationReport() markdown formatter. Integrated into plan handler: runs after research, writes validation-report.md, prompts user on critical issues (revise/override/cancel). 44 new tests. 447 total tests passing, zero typecheck errors.

userStories.title: Epic 5.5: Non-Software Domain Planning
userStories.description: As a domain expert, I want /bp:plan to clearly distinguish between [HUMAN] manual actions and [AGENT] automated actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Each task tagged as [HUMAN] or [AGENT]
Human steps include checklist format for manual tracking
System pauses at human steps and prompts user before proceeding
Progress persisted for session resume
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. classifyTask() keyword heuristic, [HUMAN]/[AGENT] tagging in plan files, clack.confirm pause for human steps, progress.json for session resume.

userStories.title: Epic 6.1: Wave-Parallel Execution with Subagent Isolation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want /bp:execute to run each wave's tasks in parallel using isolated subagents with clean context windows.
acceptanceCriteria: Tasks within same wave dispatched to independent subagents simultaneously
Each subagent receives clean context: relevant plan file + task context + codebase context only
Subagents do NOT share state or inherit orchestrator context
Next wave begins only after all current wave tasks complete
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Epic 6.2: Atomic Git Commits per Task
userStories.description: As a developer, I want every completed task to produce exactly one Git commit with standardized format type(phase-plan): description.
acceptanceCriteria: Each successful task produces exactly one Git commit
Commit message format: type(phase-plan): description
No task produces zero or multiple commits
Number of commits equals number of executed tasks
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented atomic-commit.ts with inferCommitType, formatCommitMessage, runAtomicCommit. WaveTask gains phaseSlug, TaskExecutionResult gains commitMessage. 530 tests pass.

userStories.title: Epic 6.3: Crash Recovery with Automatic Retry
userStories.description: As a developer, I want up to 3 automatic recovery strategies before escalation, with rollback to last good state.
acceptanceCriteria: Framework tracks failures and attempts up to 3 different recovery approaches
Stuck loops detected and broken with different strategy tried
After 3 failures: rollback to last good state and escalate to user with failure summary
No partial or broken changes remain in codebase after rollback
Git history reflects only successful commits
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented in src/engine/recovery.ts: selectNextStrategy (3-strategy retry chain: retry→simplify→skip), isStuckLoop (detect identical errors repeating), buildFailureSummary (markdown escalation report), handleTaskFailure (immutable session updates with exhaustion detection), createRecoverySession (captures git HEAD), executeRollback (git reset --hard). 30 unit tests. 560 total tests passing.

userStories.title: Epic 6.4: Goal-Backward Wave Verification
userStories.description: As a developer, I want each wave's output verified against relevant spec ACs before proceeding to the next wave.
acceptanceCriteria: Pass/fail report generated per AC relevant to completed wave
100% pass rate required before next wave begins
Failed criteria block progression
Targeted fix plan auto-generated for failed criteria
Fix plan executable via /bp:execute without full pipeline re-run
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Epic 6.5: Budget Guards
userStories.description: As a tech lead, I want hard spending limits at session/phase/day levels that pause execution immediately when reached.
acceptanceCriteria: Budget limits configurable in config.yaml: per_session_usd, per_phase_usd, per_day_usd
Execution pauses immediately when limit reached with cost summary
User offered: increase limit, switch to cheaper profile, or stop and preserve results
No further AI calls until user makes a choice
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented: readBudgetConfig, checkBudget, formatCostSummary, readDailySpend, updateDailySpend, writeBudgetLimit. Integrated into execute handler with 3-option user prompt. 27 unit tests added.

userStories.title: Epic 7.1: Guided Acceptance Test (UAT)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want /bp:verify to walk me through each AC from the spec with structured pass/fail test.
acceptanceCriteria: /bp:verify loads active spec.md and presents each AC one at a time
Guides what to check, expected outcome, prompts PASS/FAIL
Structured verification report generated with all criteria PASS/FAIL status
Verified spec marked and session logged to .buildpact/audit/
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Epic 7.2: Automatic Fix Plan Generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want failed verification items to auto-generate targeted fix plans executable without re-running the full pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Fix plan auto-generated targeting only failed ACs
Fix plan does not re-plan entire feature
/bp:execute on fix plan re-executes only failing items
/bp:verify can re-run after fixes to confirm
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Epic 7.3: Memory Layer Tier 1 — Session Feedback
userStories.description: As a developer, I want verification sessions to auto-capture structured feedback files in .buildpact/memory/feedback/ with FIFO cap of 30 entries.
acceptanceCriteria: Feedback files created in .buildpact/memory/feedback/ after verification
Captures: what worked, what failed, session outcome
FIFO cap of 30 entries per feedback file enforced
5 most recent feedback files auto-loaded into new session subagent context
Memory Layer free in open-source core (FR-804)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented session-feedback.ts engine module with FIFO cap=30, loadRecentFeedbacks (5 files), formatFeedbackForContext. Integrated into verify handler. 32 new tests. 682 total tests passing.

userStories.title: Epic 7.4: Memory Layer Tier 2 — Lessons & Patterns (Beta)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want recurring successful patterns distilled into lessons files for systematic reuse across sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Lessons files created in .buildpact/memory/lessons/ from recurring feedback patterns
Processing triggered manually or after 5 sessions
Relevant lessons loaded alongside feedback during planning
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Beta milestone scope

userStories.title: Epic 7.5: Memory Layer Tier 3 — Decisions Log (v1.0)
userStories.description: As a tech lead, I want key architectural decisions saved to .buildpact/memory/decisions/ for permanent reference by future agent sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Decisions files created with: decision, rationale, alternatives considered, date
Decisions log available as reference context for planning agents
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: v1.0 milestone scope

userStories.title: Epic 8.1: Squad Scaffolding & Installation
userStories.description: As a domain expert, I want to scaffold a new Squad or install an existing one with a single command.
acceptanceCriteria: npx buildpact squad create <name> generates 4-tier hierarchy template with all 6 layers, squad.yaml, benchmark/
Scaffold includes inline documentation explaining each layer
npx buildpact squad add <name> downloads, validates (structural + security), and installs to .buildpact/squads/
Warning displayed for unreviewed community Squads
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-03-16. scaffoldSquad (4-tier T1-T4 agents + 6 layers + bench/), validateSquadStructure, validateSquadSecurity, installSquad. 40 new tests, 789 total.

userStories.title: Epic 8.2: 6-Layer Agent Definition
userStories.description: As a Squad creator, I want an enforced 6-layer anatomy template (identity, persona, voice_dna, heuristics, examples×3, handoffs) for each agent.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent template contains all 6 layers with inline documentation and examples
Missing layers reported as errors during Squad validation with specific agent/layer reference
Heuristics include IF/THEN rules with veto conditions
Examples require minimum 3 concrete input/output pairs
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-03-16. Added heuristics validation: min 3 IF/THEN rules (When/If numbered items) + at least one VETO: keyword. 4 new tests. 793 total tests passing.

userStories.title: Epic 8.3: Voice DNA Creation with 5-Section Template
userStories.description: As a Squad creator, I want a mandatory 5-section Voice DNA template with a step-by-step creation guide.
acceptanceCriteria: Voice DNA requires 5 sections: Personality Anchors, Opinion Stance, Anti-Patterns (min 5 pairs), Never-Do Rules, Inspirational Anchors
Omitting any section causes Squad validation to fail
Step-by-step Voice DNA creation guide available in documentation
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-03-16. Added Anti-Patterns min 5 pairs validation (✘ count) to validateSquadStructure. Updated test validAgentContent to 5 pairs. Added 3 new tests. Created docs/voice-dna-guide.md with step-by-step guide. 796 total tests passing.

userStories.title: Epic 8.4: Squad Structural Validation
userStories.description: As a Squad creator, I want /bp:squad validate to run comprehensive structural and security checks.
acceptanceCriteria: /bp:squad validate checks: 6-layer completeness, Voice DNA 5-section, heuristic coverage, example quality (min 3), handoff graph validity
Community Squads also get security checks: no external URLs, no executable code, no path violations, no prompt injection
Detailed PASS/FAIL report with specific references
Community Squads blocked until all security checks pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed in Epic 8.4. validateHandoffGraph added to squad-scaffolder.ts; runValidate added to squad handler with --community flag for security checks; 19 new i18n keys (en + pt-br); 10 new tests; 806 tests passing.

userStories.title: Epic 8.5: Agent Autonomy Leveling System (L1-L4)
userStories.description: As a Squad creator, I want agents with autonomy levels L1-L4 that evolve based on approval/rejection track record.
acceptanceCriteria: New agents default to L1 (Observer) or L2 (Contributor)
System suggests promotion after 7 days with >85% approval rate
System suggests demotion if >30% rejection in rolling 7-day window
Promotion/demotion requires explicit user confirmation
L1 agents always require user confirmation for write operations
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed in Epic 8.5. autonomy-manager.ts engine with L1-L4 levels, approval/rejection tracking, promotion/demotion suggestions, requiresWriteConfirmation; L1 confirmation integrated into execute handler; 43 new tests; 849 tests passing.

userStories.title: Epic 8.6: Lazy Agent Loading (v2.0)
userStories.description: As a developer in Agent Mode, I want Squad agents loaded on-demand rather than all at once to stay within context budget.
acceptanceCriteria: Only Chief agent + lightweight index (≤1KB) loaded initially in Agent Mode
Specialists loaded on-demand during handoffs, unloaded after completion
Prompt Mode includes documented guide for manual agent loading best practices
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed: lazy-agent-loader.ts with buildAgentIndex/loadChiefAgent/loadSpecialistAgent/releaseAgent/createAgentSession + formatAgentIndex (<=1KB) + 33 tests + docs/prompt-mode-agent-loading-guide.md

userStories.title: Epic 9.1: Software Squad — Reference Implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Software Squad pre-installed with PM, Architect, Developer, QA, and Tech Writer agents following 6-layer anatomy.
acceptanceCriteria: Software Squad in .buildpact/squads/software/ with all 5 agents fully defined
Each agent follows 6-layer anatomy and Voice DNA 5-section template
Squad passes all structural validation checks out of the box
Appropriate agent engaged for each pipeline phase (specify, plan, execute)
Serves as reference implementation for community Squad creators
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. Fixed all 5 agents in templates/squads/software/ to have ≥5 ✘ Anti-Patterns and VETO condition in Heuristics. Added phases: mapping to squad.yaml. Created 41-test suite in test/unit/engine/software-squad.test.ts. 923 total tests passing.

userStories.title: Epic 9.2: Medical Marketing Squad — CFM-Compliant (Private)
userStories.description: As a medical marketing professional, I want a pre-built Squad with Strategist, Copywriter, Designer, Analytics agents enforcing CFM compliance.
acceptanceCriteria: Strategist, Copywriter, Designer, Analytics agents available
Copywriter Voice DNA enforces CFM nº 1.974/2011 — refuses prohibited claims, flags violations
Built-in CFM/ANVISA checklists, WhatsApp CTA templates, Schema JSON-LD medical templates
Compliance gate identifies CFM/ANVISA violations with specific rule references
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. templates/squads/medical-marketing/ with 4 agents (Strategist T1, Copywriter T2, Designer T2, Analytics T3). src/engine/cfm-compliance-gate.ts with checkCfmCompliance/formatComplianceReport/listTrackedRules — 6 rule sets covering CFM 1.974/2011 Arts. 6/7§1/7§2/7§3/9/14 + ANVISA RDC 96/2008. Built-in templates: cfm-checklist.md, whatsapp-cta.md (5 templates), schema-jsonld.json (MedicalOrganization+Physician+MedicalWebPage). 75 new tests (29 compliance gate + 46 squad structure). 998 total tests passing.

userStories.title: Epic 9.3: Scientific Research Squad (Private, Beta)
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want a pre-built Squad with Research Lead, Literature Reviewer, Data Analyst, Peer Reviewer, and LaTeX Writer agents.
acceptanceCriteria: All 5 agents available with systematic review protocols
PRISMA checklist templates and statistical analysis plan templates pre-loaded
Domain-aware questions address: research question, study design, inclusion/exclusion criteria, statistical approach
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Beta milestone, private

userStories.title: Epic 9.4: Clinic Management Squad (Private, Beta)
userStories.description: As a clinic manager, I want a pre-built Squad with Operations Manager, Finance Analyst, Compliance Checker, and Patient Flow Optimizer.
acceptanceCriteria: All 4 agents available
Compliance Checker validates outputs against Brazilian healthcare regulations
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Beta milestone, private

userStories.title: Epic 9.5: Agent Builder Squad — Meta Creation Tool (Public)
userStories.description: As a domain expert, I want an Agent Builder Squad that guides me through creating a production-quality custom Squad.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent Designer, Workflow Architect, Tester agents guide through: roles, Voice DNA, heuristics, examples, handoffs
Tester validates each agent against 6-layer and Voice DNA requirements during build
Completed Squad ready for /bp:squad validate
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Beta milestone, public

userStories.title: Epic 10.1: Web Bundle Export Command
userStories.description: As a Squad creator, I want /bp:export-web <platform> to generate a single copiable prompt for Claude.ai/ChatGPT/Gemini.
acceptanceCriteria: /bp:export-web generates .txt bundle with: compressed Squad agents, Constitution essentials, project context, workflow instructions
Token count estimate included; warning at 80% of platform limit (180K Claude, 128K ChatGPT, 1M Gemini)
Non-technical user can paste into Claude.ai and activate Squad workflow in configured language
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Epic 10.2: Progressive Bundle Compression
userStories.description: As a Squad creator, I want deterministic compression to stay within platform token limits with tiered degradation.
acceptanceCriteria: Compression sequence: inline active agents → compress Constitution → exclude optimization/memory → current context only
If still over limit: tiered degradation (remove examples → remove heuristic detail → Chief-only → minimal quick-session)
Each degradation tier documented inside bundle
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Epic 10.3: Conversational Interface Adaptation
userStories.description: As a non-technical end-user, I want Web Bundle interactions to be natural language conversations with no commands or jargon.
acceptanceCriteria: All slash commands replaced with natural language conversation flows in Web Bundle
Options presented as numbered choices in natural language
No technical terms (repository, branch, commit, YAML, pipeline, subagent) in user-facing conversation
PT-BR bundle uses Portuguese with no English fallback
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Epic 10.4: Bundle Versioning & Staleness Detection
userStories.description: As a non-technical user, I want bundles to warn me if outdated (>7 days or source files changed).
acceptanceCriteria: Bundle includes generation timestamp and source file hash
Bundle older than 7 days triggers warning suggesting fresh export
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Epic 10.5: Prompt-to-Agent Mode Migration Path (v2.0)
userStories.description: As a developer transitioning to Agent Mode, I want a migration command that validates and upgrades all existing artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: npx buildpact migrate-to-agent validates existing files, generates Agent Mode config, preserves Git history
Compatibility report with specific file references and remediation steps
Project continues working in Prompt Mode until Agent Mode explicitly activated
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: v2.0 milestone scope

userStories.title: Epic 11.1: Public Community Hub Repository
userStories.description: As a user, I want a buildpact-squads GitHub repo to discover and install community Squads with a single command.
acceptanceCriteria: buildpact-squads repo organized by domain with README, validation badges, MIT license per Squad
npx buildpact squad add <name> downloads, validates (structural + security), and installs
Warning for unreviewed Squads
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Epic 11.2: Squad Contribution Flow with Automated CI
userStories.description: As a Squad creator, I want a clear fork-create-validate-PR contribution flow with automated CI checks.
acceptanceCriteria: CI runs structural validation and security checks on PR
CI results posted as PR check summaries with specific pass/fail details
Merged Squads immediately available via npx buildpact squad add
Failed CI includes specific messages and suggested fixes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Epic 11.3: Community Health & Governance Infrastructure
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want CONTRIBUTING.md (bilingual), good-first-issue labels, and ADRs for community sustainability.
acceptanceCriteria: CONTRIBUTING.md in PT-BR and EN with step-by-step instructions
≥5 good-first-issue labels with clear scope, <2hr effort, assigned mentor
ADRs in docs/decisions/ following MADR template for significant decisions
PR template enforces bilingual i18n strings for user-facing text
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Epic 12.0: AutoResearch Architecture Decision Record (ADR-001)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ADR-001 documenting AutoResearch isolation architecture before any code is written.
acceptanceCriteria: ADR-001 created at docs/decisions/ADR-001-autoResearch-isolation.md
Documents: problem, alternatives, chosen approach (isolated Git branch), consequences, open questions
ADR approved before any Story 12.1+ implementation begins
Deviations require ADR update and re-approval
userStories.notes: Pre-requisite for all other Epic 12 stories

userStories.title: Epic 12.1: AutoResearch Command & Program File
userStories.description: As an expert developer, I want /bp:optimize <target> with a program.md defining goal, constraints, and acceptance criteria.
acceptanceCriteria: /bp:optimize launches autonomous experimentation loop on target file
program.md defines: optimization goal, constraints, experiment directions, acceptance criteria
Command available only at Expert experience level
Target files >600 lines blocked with shard-first instruction
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Epic 12.2: Fixed-Budget Experiment Loop
userStories.description: As an expert user, I want each experiment on a 5-minute budget and total session configurable up to 24 hours.
acceptanceCriteria: Individual experiment: 5-minute default time budget (configurable)
Total session: 30-minute default (configurable up to 24 hours)
Budget exhaustion stops loop cleanly, preserves all results
Budget Guard integration: loop pauses at cost limit with continue/stop options
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Epic 12.3: Git Ratchet — Commit Only Proven Improvements
userStories.description: As an expert user, I want the framework to auto-commit only when metrics improve and revert everything else.
acceptanceCriteria: Improved: commit with format optimize(N): description | metric: X.XX → Y.YY
Equal or worse: git-revert to last successful state automatically
Independent TypeScript module at src/optimize/ratchet.ts with ≥80% Vitest coverage
All operations on isolated branch: optimize/{target-type}/{session-name}/{timestamp}
Human review required before merge to main — no auto-merge
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Epic 12.4: Domain-Specific Metrics
userStories.description: As an expert user, I want built-in metrics for code/marketing/agents plus custom metric support via executable scripts.
acceptanceCriteria: Software metrics: test pass rate, bundle size, Lighthouse score, build time, coverage, type-check
Marketing metrics: readability score, compliance pass rate, keyword density, CTA clarity (evaluator ≠ generator model)
Custom metrics: any executable returning numeric score to stdout
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Epic 12.5: Optimization Report
userStories.description: As an expert user, I want an auto-generated optimization-report.md summarizing experiments, improvements, and diffs.
acceptanceCriteria: optimization-report.md contains: experiments run, improvements found, changes made, before/after metrics, diff of kept modifications
Report indicates which branch to merge and expected metric impact
results.tsv append-only log contains full experiment history
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
